CHANGE RECORD 
 
When the quality of the products changes, the QuID is updated, and a row is added to this table.   
The third column specifies which sections or sub-sections have been updated.  The fourth column should 
mention the reason for updating the document. Column 5 should include the names of the author(s) 
doing the changes. Including the name in column 6 shows that the document has been validated by the 
TAC/MFC PQ leader or deputy (or TAC/MFC PQ leader or the deputy). This validation means the 
document was checked for contents, formats and consistency with other documents.

KEY DEFINITIONS 
2 meter air temperature The air temperature measured at 2 meters height, T2m 
Sea Surface Temperature The temperature of the ocean water close to the surface. 
Sea-Ice Surface 
Temperature 
Representative of the surface temperature over sea ice. 
Thermal skin layer The layer within which the long-wave radiation penetrates the ocean, typically 
less than 1mm, where ocean -atmosphere heat exchange takes place and 
temperature is measured using Infra-red sensors. 
SSTsub-skin Sea Surface Temperature at the base of the thermal skin layer. 
SSTskin Measurement of the sea surface temperature at the very top layer, or "skin," 
of the ocean. 
SSTfoundation Sea Surface Temperature at the base of the thermal layer, which is considered 
representative of night -time and/or well mixed conditions, i.e , free of any 
diurnal signal.
